body:
Steve,

You asked if I was still keeping the same morning setup — yeah, nothing's changed. Up around 5:30, coffee going before I'm dressed, Herald off the porch by the time I'm at the table. They ran a decent piece this morning on infrastructure projects coming into Allegheny County. Couple of union shops mentioned. I cut it out; might be worth sharing with the advanced class as a real-world context piece.

Leaving for Three Rivers by 7:30 like always. Sixteen students this week, two starting fresh Monday. Lesson plan's ready.

Don't fix what isn't broken.

—Dad

Dad,

Glad the routine's holding. Honestly wasn't worried — you've had that same morning locked in longer than I've been alive.

The infrastructure piece sounds worth passing along. If there's actual regional work coming in, those kids should know about it before they're even done with the program. Good thinking.

Are the two new ones coming in with any prior shop experience, or starting from zero? Just curious what you're working with.

Talk soon,
Steve

Stevie,

That reminder app you set up worked fine Tuesday and Wednesday. Thursday it didn't fire until 7:45 — I was already backing out of the driveway. Had to sit in the truck and pull up the roster on my phone like some kind of millennial.

I need the alert at 6:45, not 7:00. By the time I've had coffee and gone through the Herald there's still the lesson plan to review before I lock up. Seven o'clock doesn't leave enough buffer, especially if I've got a complicated day — cert class plus the intro safety module don't run themselves.

Otherwise it's working. Just fix the time.

— Dad

Dad,

My fault on the timing — I had the wrong offset set when I configured it. Fixed now, 6:45 starting Monday. You'll get the roster summary and lesson plan reminder together so it's one thing to look at, not two.

I also added a flag that highlights any student who's missed more than two sessions in the last three weeks. Figured that might save you a step in the morning without making you dig through the whole list. Let me know if that's useful or if it's just clutter and I'll pull it.

Should be solid by Sunday night. Check it Monday before you head out and text me if anything's off.

— Steve

Stevie,

The absence flag is useful. Keep it.

Checked it this morning — roster loaded clean, reminder hit at 6:45 on the dot. Made it out the door by 7:20, which is the first time this week my knees and I have been ahead of schedule at the same time.

Monday class went fine. Cert group is sharp this term. I've got bowling tonight at Liberty Lanes so I'll be unreachable after six, but if something breaks with the system just leave a note and I'll look at it Tuesday before I leave.

Good work.

— Dad

Steve,

Spring roster for the welding program finally came through from admin — 14 students, two of them transfers from Pittsburgh Tech Center. I'm set for Monday but the safety certification paperwork for those two transfers hasn't cleared yet. If they show up Tuesday without it, I can't put them on the floor — that's not a judgment call, that's OSHA.

Can you check with Cheryl in enrollment and find out what the hold-up is? I'm at the academy Mon through Thu, 8 to 3, so reach me before 7:30 in the morning or after 3:30 in the afternoon.

— Dad

Dad,

Talked to Cheryl. The transfer paperwork is stuck waiting on faxed transcripts from Pittsburgh Tech Center — their fax machine has been down since last week. She's switching to email and expects to have clearance by end of day Monday.

If it's not resolved by Tuesday morning, she can issue a temporary exemption so both students observe only — no floor access — until the docs are confirmed. She wanted to know if that arrangement works for you before she drafts it.

— Steve

Steve,

Observation-only works fine for Tuesday if it comes to that. I'd rather have them watching proper arc technique from behind the line than sitting in the break room for two days. Tell Cheryl that's acceptable and to go ahead.

Separate issue — the exhaust fan in bay two has been making a grinding noise again. I put in a work order back in February, nothing happened. If there's a facilities contact I can reach directly, send me the name. I'm not running students in that bay with ventilation I can't trust.

— Dad

Dad,

I forwarded the bay two issue to Mike Stanton in facilities — he's the direct contact, and I cc'd him on the message to Cheryl. He said he'll have someone look at it Monday before 8 AM.

Cheryl has the observation exemption ready to issue. She'll email you directly Monday morning so you know the status before you leave the house.

You're set for the week. Let me know if anything else comes up.

— Steve

Steve,

Doing the Friday rundown. Next week is straight teaching all four days — leaving by 7:30, back by mid-afternoon. I've got a new group starting arc welding fundamentals Monday, so I need to pull the shop ventilation checklist over the weekend and make sure I've got enough rod stock. Don't want to be scrambling at 7 AM.

Saturday: Costco on Route 51 and the hardware store next to it. I'm going early, before the crowds hit. Also need to swing by your grandma Mary's — she's been on me for two weeks about that garage door. I keep bumping it and I feel bad about it.

Linda says she and Kevin might do Sunday dinner but nothing's confirmed. She said she'd let me know, so I figure I'll hear from Kevin Friday night.

Anything you need from Costco, send me a list tonight.

— Dad

Dad,

I'll come with you Saturday if you're okay with that. Can be at the house by 8. We can knock out Grandma Mary's garage door on the way back — easier with two people anyway.

Send me the ventilation checklist if you want a second set of eyes before Monday. I know you've got it handled but the offer stands.

I texted Linda. She said Sunday is a yes, 5 o'clock. Kevin's bringing something. I didn't ask what because it doesn't matter.

I don't need anything from Costco. That place is a trap and I'm staying out of it.

— Steve

Steve,

Followed up on that Gmail API thing you walked me through last week. Got it authorized okay but now when I run the script it throws a 403 and says something about insufficient permissions. Copied the error below.

"Request had insufficient authentication scopes. [403]"

I went back into Google Cloud and stared at the scopes screen for ten minutes. Didn't help. I'm pretty sure I need to add something in there but I don't want to blow up what's already working.

I'm out the door by 7:30 Monday through Thursday so if you can shoot me something I can read Sunday night that'd be good. Not urgent — just want to get this running before I forget what I set up.

— Dad

Dad,

Yeah, that 403 means the OAuth token you have was generated before you added the right scope. The token's cached but it's missing permissions — you have to delete it and re-authorize so Google issues a new one with the correct scope.

Here's what to do:
1. Find the token file on your machine — it's usually called token.json in whatever folder you ran the script from.
2. Delete that file.
3. Run the script again. It'll open the browser and ask you to sign in.
4. This time make sure you see it asking for Gmail permissions, not just basic profile stuff. Click Allow.

Also double-check in Google Cloud Console under "OAuth consent screen" → "Scopes" that you have https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly (or .modify if you need to send/move things). If that scope isn't listed there, add it first, then delete the token and re-auth.

Should take five minutes. Let me know what you get.

— Steve

Steve,

That was it. Deleted the token file, ran it again, browser popped up, clicked through — this time it actually listed Gmail in the permissions. Working now.

I'll be honest, I stared at that token.json file last week and didn't touch it because I figured it was doing something important. Good to know it's just a key you can throw out and get a new one.

Thanks for the walkthrough. Gave me enough time Sunday to actually prep my Monday roster, which is all I was really after. Appreciate it.

— Dad

Steve,

Tried setting up the Google Calendar sync like you walked me through last week. API key is in, credentials look fine, but it's pulling in events from last semester. My Mon–Thu class blocks at Three Rivers are showing correctly, but the Friday planning sessions aren't populating at all. Those are the ones I use to prep the next week's roster and lesson plan — I need those.

Also, Monday bowling nights aren't showing up. Liberty Lanes, 7 to 9, been a recurring event for two years. I don't know why the sync would drop those.

I've still got the paper calendar on the wall so it's not an emergency. But let me know when you get a chance.

Dad

Dad,

The missing Friday and Monday events are almost certainly a timezone issue. The Google Calendar API defaults to UTC unless you explicitly pass a timezone in the request — something like `timeZone: 'America/New_York'`. Without it, events near the day boundary can shift or disappear entirely depending on how the sync script handles the offset.

The old semester events pulling in are a separate thing. There's a `timeMin` parameter that sets the earliest date for returned events. If it's blank or not set, the API returns everything going back to whenever the calendar was created.

If you can get me access to the script — Google Apps Script editor, or whoever set it up — I can fix both in about ten minutes. Otherwise send me a screenshot of the API response and I'll go from there.

Steve

Steve,

Found the script in the Apps Script editor. Took me longer to find where that was than it did to read the thing. Forwarded it to your Gmail.

The timeMin was blank, so I put in the first day of this semester like you said. Friday planning sessions are showing up now. Still missing the Monday bowling nights — turns out those are on a separate 'Personal' calendar, not the main one. I'm guessing the sync is only reading the primary calendar and ignoring that one entirely.

Knees weren't thrilled about the extra time at the desk but we got there.

Dad

Dad,

Yep, that's exactly it. By default the API only reads the primary calendar. To get the Personal calendar included, you'd either add a second `calendarId` to the API call or consolidate the events. Easier route: move the bowling series to your primary calendar. Open one of the Monday events, choose 'Edit all events in series,' and switch the calendar dropdown from Personal to your main one. No code changes needed.

Script otherwise looks clean — and you got the timezone fix right on the first try, which is more than I can say for most people I walk through this.

Steve

Steve,

Following up on my ticket from last week about the WhatsApp Business API setup. Haven't heard back and I've got a department meeting Thursday where I was hoping to show the director something concrete.

Basically what I'm trying to do is push shop-floor safety reminders and schedule changes to students through WhatsApp instead of printing half a ream of paper every Monday. We've got around 40 active students across two cohorts. Not trying to build anything fancy — just outbound messages, maybe a confirmation reply.

I'm only reachable by email Mon through Thursday before 7:30 AM or after 3 PM. Fridays are my planning day so response time is better then. If there's a form I missed or a verification step still pending on your end, just point me at it.

— Steve Petersen
Three Rivers Trade Academy

Mr. Petersen,

Apologies for the delay. I pulled up your original ticket and found it was held in our business verification queue — we needed a utility bill or official document showing the academy's registered address before we could move forward. That step didn't trigger an automated reminder, which is on us.

For 40 users you'd qualify for the Cloud API through Meta's standard tier — no monthly fee at that volume, just per-message costs after a free threshold. I've re-opened your ticket and attached a one-page checklist of what to upload. Once that's in, verification typically takes 2–3 business days.

If Thursday is your deadline, submitting the documents today gives you a reasonable shot. I'll monitor the ticket personally and flag it if anything else gets held up. You can reply here or hit me directly on this thread.

— Steve P. III
Customer Support

Steve,

Following up on that Google Drive API thing we talked about last week. IT at Three Rivers finally set up the shared folder for lesson plans and cert prep docs, and I still can't get the script you sent to pull files without throwing an auth error. Tried it again this morning before I had to leave at 7:30 — same result every time.

Error says invalid_grant. I copied the credentials.json exactly the way you wrote it out. Folder's got around 40 files in it — safety handouts, welding certification prep stuff, that kind of thing. All things I need Monday morning.

Let me know what I'm doing wrong.

Dad

Dad,

invalid_grant almost always means the OAuth token expired or it's pointing at the wrong account. Try this first: find the token.json file in the same folder as the script and delete it. Then re-run — it'll open a browser window and ask you to log in again. That resets everything.

When it asks which account, make sure you're signing in with the Three Rivers account, not your personal Gmail. The API will only work with whatever account you used during the original setup, and it has a habit of defaulting to whichever one the browser has open.

If deleting token.json doesn't do it, check that the laptop clock is synced — Google rejects tokens if the system time is off by more than a couple minutes. Settings > Date & Time > set it to sync automatically.

Let me know what happens.

Steve

Steve,

Deleting the token file did it. Re-ran the script, browser opened, logged in with the Three Rivers account, and it pulled everything clean. Forty-three files, all there.

Donnie was over Friday for coffee and I mentioned it — turns out he's been fighting the same thing with his shop's file setup for months. Told him to reach out to you.

Good timing. I'm building out next week's safety module and needed those cert prep docs before Monday. We're good.

Dad
